channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hungary's transformation into an 'electoral autocracy' has parallels to Trump's second term
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hours before President Donald Trump was sworn in to begin his second term, promising a “golden age” for America, the leader of a Central European country was describing the years ahead in strikingly similar terms.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Legislative immunity is a privilege in most states. A speeding ticket could change that in Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — When a police officer gave state Sen. Mark Finchem a speeding ticket, the Arizona Republican didn't need to worry about the consequences.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss N.Y.C. Mayor Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Fearful immigrants ask Florida activist to sign guardianship papers for their children
item.description: Since President Donald Trump took office, immigrants in the U.S. illegally have changed their travel patterns and try to stay home, out of fear they could be deported.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department asks court to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department asked a court Friday to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, with a top official from Washington intervening after federal prosecutors in Manhattan rebuffed his demands to drop the case and some quit in protest.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Treasury watchdog begins audit of Musk DOGE team's access to the US government's payment system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department's Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government's payment system, as Democratic lawmakers raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk's Department of Government
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Denny's to close dozens more restaurants in 2025
item.description: Denny's was already planning to close dozens of locations this year. Now it's planning to close even more.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Alleged DOGE employees show up at San Francisco City Hall
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- An official with the San Francisco Sheriff’s Office confirmed to NewsNation affiliate KRON4 that it is investigating a report of three individuals claiming to be Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) employees who attempted to enter offices in San Francisco City Hall on
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: MISSION, Texas (AP) — An idled Border Patrol bus sat empty this week, on standby for any migrants surrendering near the southern tip of Texas. Agents in two speedboats zipped past pockets of sandy shores, known landing spots for people entering the U.S.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 people shot in Philadelphia as city celebrates Eagles victory
item.description: Two people were shot in Philadelphia Friday afternoon as the city held its Eagles Super Bowl Parade, Philadelphia Police said. The shooting happened outside of the parade footprint, police said.
item.dc:creator: Jared Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers deliver gifts to widows on Valentine's Day. The project began in a florist's driveway
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jillian Myers knows the devastation of losing a husband and feeling alone and empty, particularly on Valentine's Day.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump border czar and NYC mayor interview with 'Fox & Friends' on immigration commitments
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Reestablishing an immigration office at New York's notorious Rikers Island jail is just the first step in a more complex agreement President Donald Trump's administration has reached with Democratic Mayor Eric Adams, the new U.S.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Park Service removes transgender references from LGBTQ historical site's website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KTLA 5

item.title: US Park Service removes transgender references from Stonewall National Monument website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Fox News embeds with ICE pursuing targets, including illegal migrant wanted for sexual assault conviction
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement's (ICE) Enforcement and Removal Operations in Houston gave Fox News an inside look at day-to-day operations, as officers make detentions and target illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CBP officer pleads not guilty to migrant smuggling, drug conspiracy
item.description: A U.S. Customs and Border Protection officer charged with multiple counts of violating immigration and drug laws has pleaded not guilty to the charges, court documents show.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Cause of death for 'General Hospital' actress revealed
item.description: Former “General Hospital” actress Leslie Charleson’s death last month was the result of “sequelae of blunt head trauma,” according to the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner’s Office. Charleson, 79, had died on Jan. 12, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: WATCH: Driver in Utah escapes car before train smashes into vehicle
item.description: Video appears to show that the SUV may have been pushed onto the tracks by another vehicle, just as the crossing gates were beginning to lower.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S. to abide by a law that required TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the platform or face a Jan. 19 ban.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration begins sweeping layoffs with probationary workers, warns of larger cuts to come
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Thursday intensified its sweeping efforts to shrink the size of the federal workforce, the nation's largest employer, by ordering agencies to lay off nearly all probationary employees who had not yet gained civil service protection — potentially affectin
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mother of Little Rock homicide victim said son was killed while trying to sell shoes on Facebook Marketplace
item.description: During the last week of January, four people were killed in Little Rock, including a 19-year-old selling shoes on Facebook Marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Gary Burton Jr., NewsNation

item.title: Florida man is executed for the 1997 killing of a couple whose toddler witnessed the attack
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a husband and wife at a remote farm in an attack witnessed by the couple’s toddler was put to death Thursday in the state's first execution of the year. James Dennis Ford, 64, was pronounced dead at 6:19 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s move to slash research funding shakes medical community
item.description: Efforts by the Trump administration to drastically cut federal investments in medical research are threatening to kneecap major research institutions and stifle scientific progress to combat chronic illnesses.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: More than 500 JOANN stores expected to close, including some in San Diego County
item.description: When JOANN filed for bankruptcy last month, the company said it expected all of its stores to remain open.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Alleged serial shoplifter banned from every Walmart in the USA
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A serial shoplifter has gotten herself banned from every Walmart in the United States of America due to her history of stealing from the company, Memphis Police say. Ashley Cross, 37, is charged with criminal trespass and theft of merchandise less than $1,000.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk appears at White House defending DOGE's work but acknowledging mistakes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's most powerful adviser, Elon Musk, made a rare public appearance at the White House on Tuesday to defend the swift and extensive cuts he's pushing across the federal government while acknowledging there have been mistakes and will be more.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4
